codeReview.summary: The feature improves landing-page CTAs, adds onboarding banners, and adds a breadcrumb to the new-opportunity page, but it does not fully satisfy the navigation-completeness requirement for portal nav bars.

issues.description: Business sidebar/nav is still missing a direct link to the key `/business/profile` page. The feature explicitly required ensuring all key pages are present in student/business nav and adding missing links; this nav array only includes Dashboard, Projects, New Project, and Resources.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `fullstack-worker` skill states lint is part of required automated verification, but this worker run only shows type-check and build. This mismatch is recurring and can cause inconsistent `followedProcedure` reporting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure §4) requires `npm run lint`; transcript skeleton for session `41cfc7a6-4791-40da-87ad-f2140aeb77d9` shows `npm run type-check` and `npm run build` but no lint command.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is no shared-state definition of what constitutes 'key pages' for each portal nav, which likely contributed to leaving `/business/profile` out of the nav list despite this feature's explicit requirement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS and .factory/library/architecture.md document auth/data conventions but not nav completeness rules; feature contract in mission features specifies nav completeness for student/business layouts while `src/app/business/layout.tsx` nav omits `/business/profile` (profile appears only as a top-right account link).

summary: Fail with one blocking issue: business portal navigation remains incomplete relative to feature requirements because `/business/profile` is not included in the nav bar/sidebar links.
